chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: Guys, in today's video, I will tell you the difference between instance variables and class variables.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: We use instance variables when we have to associate any property with a particular object.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: And we use class variables when we have to make a variable for the entire class, which all the instances will share.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: Now I will tell you what I have said in this video.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: So guys, now we are going to talk about the difference between instance and class variables.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: But before that, I would like to tell you why we use object-oriented programming.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: So basically what does it do?

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: First of all, we have already talked about the classes that are in real-world entities.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: They map things to us.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: Along with that, we can make a group logically.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: We can make a group of functions, variables, and a logical group in which things are related.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: For example, if there is an employee, then all the methods related to the employee are in the employee class.

chunks.title: Instance variables vs Class variables in Python
chunks.text: After that, all the variables related to the employee are in the employee class.

chunks.text: So in this way, we can make a class called employee.
